activation_manager.handoff_summary: Activated existing multi-model stub into an official JSON-first packet after operator directed Activation Manager protocol use. Governance scope intentionally limited to spec-current alignment, taskboard, traceability, packet, microtasks, worktree, and recovery state.

refinement.operator_request: The original spec was written before modern swarm models/subagents. Use Activation Manager protocol and create a WP and worktree with MTs, keeping only enough governance for taskboard/spec/current WP/MT status and recovery.

approved_spec_enrichment.summary: Add ModelLaneRun, ModelLane, and ModelLaneMessage as the normative cloud/local/subagent exchange contract.
Add CX-MM-006..009 failstate codes for missing lane payloads, cloud consent/projection gaps, unauthorized peer channels, and orphaned subagent lanes.

scope_edges: Activate the existing v1 stub identity; do not invent a new WP name.
Keep repo-governance ceremony minimal because repo governance is planned for abandonment.
Allow operator ad hoc MT additions and changes after activation.
Implementation must reuse existing ModelRuntime, cloud lane, SwarmCoordinator, SessionBroker, ContextBundle, ArtifactStore/EventLedger, and diagnostics where practical.

Product implementation scope is Rust-only for this WP: Rust backend plus Rust native egui/Argus surfaces. app/src legacy React/Tauri/TypeScript surfaces are reference-only and must not be implementation or proof authority.
Every MT implementation must keep Flight Recorder/EventLedger authority, CRDT proposal/promotion, Loom, Locus, FEMS, and UserManual compatibility explicit where touched.
Palmistry/internal_diagnostics are required diagnostic posture fields through HBR-INT-009; if not present in this worktree, record DEFERRED-with-reason and do not claim live integration.

All implementation/proof surfaces for this WP are Rust-only: Rust backend plus Rust native egui/Argus. TypeScript/React/app/src/app-src-tauri/npm/docs-model-manual are reference-only or forbidden as proof authority.
Implementation MTs must use exact Rust proof targets that touch real PostgreSQL/EventLedger or the named Handshake-owned resource; zero matching tests are a hard failure.
MT lifecycle/status signaling must support Kernel Builder/Coder Spec-Realism states and restart recovery without chat history.
assumptions: The worktree branch can be based on main while the live .GOV junction points back to the governance kernel.
Initial MT status should be PENDING until a coder/subagent claims work.
The old DOCS_ONLY/AI_ENABLED stub remains historical input, not execution authority.
non_goals: No product implementation in this activation pass.
No new peer-to-peer model bus outside Handshake authority surfaces.
acceptance_criteria: Taskboard maps the packet to Ready for Dev and the old stub is no longer the active row.
Traceability registry maps the base WP to the official packet folder.
Spec current bundle contains the ModelLane exchange contract and matching hash metadata.
Worktree exists for implementation and links back to the live .GOV kernel.
MT contracts are present, PENDING, and detailed enough for ad hoc assignment.
MT contracts include the UserManual currency pass that reflects every WP behavior into the internal manual with Flight Recorder/EventLedger and Palmistry/internal_diagnostics posture.
Each MT contains research_basis and Handshake compatibility translation tied to master spec/product code authority.
Each product implementation MT requires same-change UserManual updates with Flight Recorder/EventLedger linkage and HBR-INT-009 diagnostic posture.
MTs forbid hidden peer-to-peer model channels, prompt-only handoff, SQLite authority, legacy app/src authority leaks, and npm/JavaScript proof targets.
microtask_plan: MT-003: Unified launch adapters for local, cloud, CLI, human, subagent, validator lanes
MT-004: Coordinator routing and artifact promotion
MT-005: ContextBundle handoff and model-to-model exchange
MT-006: Consent, projection, and authority boundaries
MT-007: Recovery, orphan handling, and diagnostics
MT-009: Validation harness and negative cases
MT-010: Model manual and backend navigation
MT-011: Internal UserManual currency and Flight Recorder/Palmistry linkage for every WP behavior

research_basis.summary: Research and product-code inspection strengthened MT contracts around Rust backend execution, PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, CRDT proposal/promotion, Flight Recorder/internal_diagnostics/Palmistry diagnostics, Loom/Locus/FEMS/UserManual compatibility, and deterministic model-lane orchestration.
research_basis.translation_rule: External frameworks are field evidence only; Handshake remains layered as models propose edits, Handshake validates/promotes deterministically, PostgreSQL/EventLedger stores authority, Flight Recorder records business evidence, and UserManual/Loom/Locus/FEMS make state usable by no-context models.

acceptance: Every MT has restartable lifecycle/status fields for claim, ready-for-validation, validator completion, blockers, missing resource, proof/HBR status, KB ready checklist, and runtime/recovery refs.
Rust-only proof targets replace npm, app/src, docs/model-manual, and grep-as-proof surfaces.
MT dependencies ensure consent, recovery, diagnostics, navigation, and manual audit run after the product behavior they inspect.
Pre-implementation adversarial review hardens the contracts; post-implementation adversarial review hardens each actual MT diff before READY_FOR_VALIDATION.

risks: A broad swarm WP can sprawl into unrelated governance rebuild work.
Legacy multi-model wording can be mistaken for sufficient modern subagent design.
Cloud/local lane exchange can accidentally use prompt text as hidden authority.
Subagent work can become unrecoverable if lane identity is not durable.
minimum_controls: Keep activation surfaces limited to packet, MTs, taskboard, traceability, spec-current, and worktree.
Require typed lane records and messages before implementation claims communication is complete.
Require explicit artifact promotion for authority changes.
